element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn AD&D ruleset mechanics and build initial party composition before leaving Candlekeep
Gorion serves as protective guide; violent separation establishes primary narrative motivation
Initial enemies scale with party capabilities; teaches pausable real-time combat mechanics
Six-member party formation unlocks context-dependent dialogue trees and character questlines
Environmental storytelling reveals metal degradation crisis as interconnected threat
Increasingly powerful adversaries hunting protagonist; survival-based tension
Expanded through Tales of the Sword Coast expansion; unlocks strategic depth
Final battle and Tales of the Sword Coast content presents extreme difficulty scaling

typical_relief_triggers: Safe zone resting in towns or strongholds
typical_tension_triggers: Persecution by unnamed hunters seeking protagonist death
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Pausable real-time combat allows player-controlled tension modulation; exploration-driven pacing with emergent combat encounters; escalating narrative pressure from hunting adversaries

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Sword Coast expansion areas present optional explorable zones with increasing difficulty; side quests tied to NPC recruitment create branching exploration patterns

difficulty_curve.early_game: Candlekeep tutorial and immediate post-departure encounters scale to basic party formation; low enemy threat levels allow learning pausable real-time mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Iron crisis exploration introduces moderate enemy scaling; NPC recruitment and party composition becomes critical; multiple threat vectors emerge
difficulty_curve.late_game: Increasingly powerful hunters and escalating enemy difficulty; final battle presents significant mechanical and narrative challenge
notable_spikes: Violent separation from Gorion (narrative shock, gameplay transition)
Tales of the Sword Coast expansion content (difficulty spike)
Final battle revision in original game vs expansion variant

notable_level_patterns.description: Candlekeep and towns serve as safe exploration bases from which players venture into increasingly hostile wilderness; return points for resource management and dialogue-driven character development
Iron crisis serves as connective tissue between disparate exploration zones; environmental storytelling reveals crisis scope through regional exploration rather than linear narration
Context-dependent dialogue tied to specific party members gates narrative content and questlines; encourages experimentation with party composition and replay value
